item.title: On Iteration
item.author: Author
item.description: While designing Glimmer, I had to think about what forms of iteration I should bring to the language. None are strictly necessary due do having first-class functions, thanks Haskell Curry. However, we do not wish pure recursive iteration on our worst enemies. So, what are our options? While While loops are bread-and-butter to non-functional languages. They do exactly what they promise, and they do it well. However, they are not the most convenient for keeping track of indices or iterating over a range of values.

item.title: Language vs Library Pt2
item.author: Author
item.description: What is a language responsible for supplying and what should be left to the libraries? This is a question that I have been pondering when thinking of features to add to my language, Glimmer. Obviously a language had better be turing complete and convenient to work with, but where exactly does language end and library start? One idea I had to add to Glimmer is NumPy-style arrays with suitable operations baked in.

item.title: Language vs Library
item.author: Author
item.description: Lately I’ve been thinking about the relation between a language and its libraries. Often times, libraries can carry a language to massive popularity, see Python. While Python as a language has many perks, it also has very notable downsides like performance. Writing your computationally heavy libraries in pure python doesn’t happen much, people use bindings for C/C++. So, the best way to write Python sometimes is to not write Python.

item.title: Hello, World!
item.author: Author
item.description: Welcome to my site I have finally decided to make my very own website where I can show off my projects and talk my mind about everything I do related to coding. I will probably be gushing about Glimmer, my most recent project where I have been writing an interpreter for my own programming language, but I also plan on writing about school and the space as a whole. Tech As someone with experience in industry web development, I had the urge of “if you don’t build this from scratch, you’re fake”, but I decided to shrug this off since another full length project is not what I want on my plate right now.

item.title: About
item.author: Author
item.description: Hi there! I’m Daniel 👋 My Background 🏫 🎶 🎳 I am from Shawnee, Kansas, where I lived from when I was born in March of 2000 until I was about 19. I had a average but fun upbringing focusing on school activities and getting fine grades. I did band in school from 5th grade to community college, and I did varsity bowling through all of high school. 💻 🕹️ 🥱 From an early age I was interested in computers after playing lots of video games.

item.title: Projects
item.author: Author
item.description: Quick note: This is a collection of some of my bigger, more impressive, and fun projects. To see complete code and information about all of my projects, check out my GitHub. Glimmer ✨ Description and Motivation Glimmer is my own programming language. Cool, right? I decided to do this because I really enjoyed my programming language semantics course and wanted to dive deeper. To do this, I read the book Writing An Interpreter In Go by Thorsten Ball, followed along with what they did, and have been adding much on top of it.
